channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: War with Iran disrupts fertilizer exports as U.S. farmers prepare for planting season
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded:
Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.

(Image credit: Frank Morris)
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: They gave her business a lifeline, then froze all her money
item.description: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.
item.content:encoded: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Trump delivers farmers another financial blow with Iran war
item.description: Some of President Trump's policies, the latest being the war in Iran, are testing his support among farmers who are being burdened with higher costs.
item.content:encoded:
Some of President Trump's policies, the latest being the war in Iran, are testing his support among farmers who are being burdened with higher costs.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: War with Iran disrupts fertilizer exports as US farmers prepare for planting season
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike, just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike, just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: The Iran war is impacting the global economy, and Asia is particularly vulnerable
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with Martin Wolf, chief economics commentator for the Financial Times, about how the war on Iran is affecting the global economy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with Martin Wolf, chief economics commentator for the Financial Times, about how the war on Iran is affecting the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: The U.S. is a big oil exporter. So why does it import most of the oil it consumes?
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to University of Texas engineering professor Hugh Daigle about why the U.S. imports most of the oil it consumes despite being one of the world's largest oil exporters.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to University of Texas engineering professor Hugh Daigle about why the U.S. imports most of the oil it consumes despite being one of the world's largest oil exporters.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: CBS News shutters its storied radio news service after nearly a century, ending an era
item.description: The change is part of a round of layoffs at CBS News. When the radio service began operation in September 1927, it was a precursor to the entire CBS network. Today its top-of-the-hour news roundups are delivered to about 700 stations across the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The change is part of a round of layoffs at CBS News. When the radio service began operation in September 1927, it was a precursor to the entire CBS network. Today its top-of-the-hour news roundups are delivered to about 700 stations across the U.S.

(Image credit: GRS)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Federal Reserve holds benchmark interest rate steady amid wartime uncertainty
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady this week. The central bank says it's too soon to know how the wartime spike in energy prices will affect overall inflation.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady this week. The central bank says it's too soon to know how the wartime spike in energy prices will affect overall inflation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: What the release of 150 million barrels of oil means for reserve sites in Texas
item.description: The U.S. has agreed to release more than 150 million barrels of oil from its strategic petroleum reserve. What does that mean for the stockpile located in salt caverns along the Gulf Coast?
item.content:encoded: The U.S. has agreed to release more than 150 million barrels of oil from its strategic petroleum reserve. What does that mean for the stockpile located in salt caverns along the Gulf Coast?
item.dc:creator: Natalie Weber

item.title: Federal Reserve holds a key interest rate steady amid growing economic pressures
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady today. The central bank is wrestling with a lot of uncertainty with a weak job market and stubborn inflation.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady today. The central bank is wrestling with a lot of uncertainty with a weak job market and stubborn inflation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
